sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on Box 1 had `gameplay_changed: true` and `no_impact: false`. The animation shows changes in the left panel area (32,6) 19x20 and also in the grid area (8,14) 20x4.
Looking at the image, I can see that:
1. The Left H buttons are now all ON (they were all OFF before).
2. The blue border around the selected box has moved from Box 4 to Box 1.
So clicking on the boxes selects them and also changes the Left H button state!
This is a major discovery. The boxes control the Left H button state. Let me check the current Left H button state and see if it matches the pattern in Box 1.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the current Left H button state
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Left H buttons: '{lines[33][2:32]}'")

# Let me also check the grid to see if it changed
print(f"Grid row 4: '{lines[4][32:62]}'")
print(f"Grid row 8: '{lines[8][32:62]}'")
print(f"Grid row 12: '{lines[12][32:62]}'")

# Let me check the Right H button state too
print(f"Right H buttons: '{lines[33][33:61]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Let me just look at the image. The Left H buttons are:

- Button 6: OFF (gray)
